content.problemStatement:
Write a program using a 'for' loop to find and print the index of the first occurrence of the number 8 in any given list of NNN integers. If 8 is not present in the list, print -1.

Check the sample input / output below for further details.

Note that your output needs to take into account that the array is 000 indexed.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of elements in the list. The second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the index (0‑based) of the first occurrence of the number 8 in the given list, or -1 if 8 does not appear.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The list is [2,4,8,12,8]; the first 8 appears at index 2.
There is no 8 in the list, so the answer is -1.
The single element is 8, which is at index 0.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the array from left to right using a simple for loop. The moment we encounter the value 8 we record its index and stop scanning because we need the first occurrence only. If the loop finishes without finding 8 we output -1. This is optimal because any solution must look at each element until the first 8 is found, giving O(N) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) – each element is examined at most once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input array.
